dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7068 IH: No Convicts Running the Capital Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Mace (for herself and Mr. Burchett) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

form.official-title: To prohibit the District of Columbia government from appointing individuals convicted of crimes of violence or dangerous crimes as employees of the government or from entering into contracts with vendors who employ individuals convicted of crimes of violence or dangerous crimes, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No Convicts Running the Capital Act.

section.header: Prohibiting employment with district of columbia government of individuals convicted of violent crimes

subsection.text: An employing authority of an office of the District of Columbia government may not appoint an individual to a position in the District of Columbia government unless the individual certifies that the individual has not been finally convicted of a crime of violence or a dangerous crime.

subsection.header: Effective date; termination of current employees

paragraph.text: Subsection (a) shall apply with respect to an individual appointed to a position in the District of Columbia government after the date of the enactment of this Act.

Not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the District of Columbia shall terminate the employment of any individual who has been finally convicted of a crime of violence or a dangerous crime who holds a position in the District of Columbia government on the date of the enactment of this Act.

section.header: Prohibiting district of columbia government from entering into contracts with vendors employing individuals convicted of violent crimes

subsection.text: An office of the District of Columbia government may not enter into a contract with a vendor for the provision of goods or services unless the vendor certifies that the vendor is not a covered vendor.
In this section, a vendor is a covered vendor with respect to a contract if either of the following applies:

paragraph.text: In the case of a vendor who is an individual, the vendor has been finally convicted of a crime of violence or a dangerous crime.
In the case of a vendor who is an entity—

subparagraph.text: the vendor employs an individual who has been finally convicted of a crime of violence or a dangerous crime to provide goods or services under the contract;
an individual who has been finally convicted of a crime of violence or a dangerous crime serves as an officer or director of the vendor, including by serving on the vendor’s board of directors; or
an individual who has been finally convicted of a crime of violence or a dangerous crime has a controlling ownership interest in the vendor.

subsection.header: Effective date; termination of current contracts

paragraph.text: Subsection (a) shall apply with respect to contracts entered into after the date of the enactment of this Act.

Not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the District of Columbia shall terminate any contract with a vendor who is a covered vendor which is in effect on the date of the enactment of this Act.

section.text: In this Act, the following definitions apply:

paragraph.text: The term crime of violence has the meaning given such term in section 23–1331(4), District of Columbia Official Code, except that such term includes an offense under Federal, State, or local law which is substantially similar to an offense described in such section.

The term dangerous crime has the meaning given such term in section 23–1331(3), District of Columbia Official Code, except that such term includes an offense under Federal, State, or local law which is substantially similar to an offense described in such section.
The term finally convicted means a conviction—

subparagraph.text: which has not been appealed and is no longer appealable because the time for taking an appeal has expired; or
which has been appealed and the appeals process for which is completed.
